scenario.title: Screen Failure Following Professional Screen Protector Installation with Unexpected Adhesive Spill
scenario.description: Customer reports that after having a professional apply a tempered glass screen protector using a liquid adhesive, an unexpected amount of adhesive seeped out and reached the edge of the iPhone screen. The installer attempted to clean the excess adhesive and reinstalled the protector. Within hours, the screen became intermittently unresponsive. The customer insists there was no drop, impact, or liquid submersion, and that the incident occurred during a routine accessory installation. The customer claims accidental damage from handling due to the unexpected adhesive contact during normal usage.

service_fee.notes: Service fee applies upon approval of ADH claim.

policy_references.snippet: ADH coverage only applies to mechanical failure caused by an unexpected and unintentional external event from normal daily usage.
Coverage excludes damage caused by reckless, willful or intentional conduct associated with handling.
The customer must provide information about symptoms and causes.

scenario.reasoning: The claim hinges on interpreting whether damage caused by partial removal and reinstallation of an adhesive screen protector, which is intentional and possibly outside normal handling, qualifies as an unexpected and unintentional external event under Section 3.2. Section 4.2(ii)(b) excludes willful conduct damage, creating a tension that requires detailed customer explanation of the circumstances to resolve if this is accidental. If approved, the $49 ADH fee applies and the claim counts toward the two-event limit.
scenario.adjustments: Changed the installation method to involve a liquid adhesive, introduced an unexpected adhesive spill during professional installation, and clarified that the event occurred as part of routine accessory usage, not an intentional cosmetic modification.
scenario.risk_analysis: The ambiguity now centers on whether accidental adhesive seepage during professional installation qualifies as an 'unexpected and unintentional external event from normal daily usage' (possibly covered), or if it is excluded as 'damage caused by reckless, willful or intentional conduct' (not covered). This pushes the scenario closer to the policy boundary, as the customer's actions may be seen as reasonable use with an unforeseen result, rather than an obviously excluded intentional act.

persona.class of worker: Employee of a private not-for-profit, tax-exempt, or charitable organization
persona.education: 1 or more years of college credit, no degree
persona.household type: Married couple household, no children of the householder less than 18
persona.industry category: EDU-Other Schools And Instruction, And Educational Support Services
persona.mannerisms: Tends to tell jokes or funny stories
persona.personal time: Playing a musical instrument or reading

system_prompts.customer:
You are a policyholder filing a claim with AppleCare.
You are given a persona and a scenario to role-play as the policyholder. Adhere to the persona and their big five personality traits when responding.
You will have a conversation with an AppleCare bot to file your claim.
Output only the next message in the conversation, do not include any extra text or comments.
The conversation should be turn-taking and unique. Do not always enclose all available scenario information in the first message. Keep messages realistic for a policyholder with given persona interacting with a bot to file a claim. Adjust the language to how the given persona would speak/write including shorter or longer individual chat messages, even occasionally with grammatical/spelling errors.
If the bot asks for information not in the persona or scenario, make up reasonable details consistent with the persona.
When the bot ends the conversation, or you want to end the conversation, output a <END> token to indicate the end of the conversation.

Scenario title:
Screen Failure Following Professional Screen Protector Installation with Unexpected Adhesive Spill

Scenario description:
Customer reports that after having a professional apply a tempered glass screen protector using a liquid adhesive, an unexpected amount of adhesive seeped out and reached the edge of the iPhone screen. The installer attempted to clean the excess adhesive and reinstalled the protector. Within hours, the screen became intermittently unresponsive. The customer insists there was no drop, impact, or liquid submersion, and that the incident occurred during a routine accessory installation. The customer claims accidental damage from handling due to the unexpected adhesive contact during normal usage.

system_prompts.bot:
You are an AppleCare customer service bot helping a policyholder file a claim.
Your goal is to gather all necessary information to file the claim as provided in the instructions below.
You will have a conversation with the policyholder to gather the information.
Do NOT give any information about whether the claim is covered or not. Your job is to gather the information only. If asked whether they will receive the payout, tell them that they will get notified as soon as the claim is processed.
Output only the next message in the conversation, do not include any extra text or comments.
The conversation should be turn-taking and unique.
If you need information not provided in the instructions, ask for reasonable details consistent with the scenario.
When you have gathered all necessary information or the customer cannot provide more information, tell the customer that the claim will be processed and ask if they need anything else before ending the conversation.
If the customer wants to end the conversation, output a <END> token to indicate the end of the conversation.
Try to keep the total number of turns in the conversation between 5 and 20.
Remain professional and friendly throughout the conversation. Keep the language concise and clear.

Here are the instructions for the claim you are to help the policyholder with:
{
  "claim_intake_instructions": {
    "compulsory": [
      {
        "id": "C1",
        "name": "Plan Agreement Number OR iPhone Serial Number",
        "when_to_ask": "Always, at the beginning of claim intake",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 5",
        "notes": "Used to validate coverage eligibility."
      },
      {
        "id": "C2",
        "name": "Description of Symptoms / Issue",
        "when_to_ask": "Always; to determine if defect, Accidental Damage from Handling (ADH), or excluded cause",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(ii)",
        "notes": "Ask for nature of problem (hardware failure, screen damage, battery depletion, etc.)."
      },
      {
        "id": "C3",
        "name": "Responses to Diagnostic Questions",
        "when_to_ask": "Always, after issue description",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(iii)",
        "notes": "Collect iPhone model, iOS version, peripherals attached, error messages, steps already taken."
      }
    ],
    "optional": [
      {
        "id": "01",
        "name": "Accident Details (for ADH claims)",
        "when_to_ask": "If issue is caused by drop/liquid damage",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 3.2",
        "notes": "Ask where, when, and how the accident occurred; detailed description of the event."
      },
      {
        "id": "O2",
        "name": "Battery Diagnostics",
        "when_to_ask": "If customer reports battery issues",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 3.1",
        "notes": "Confirm battery capacity has dropped ≥50%."
      },
      {
        "id": "O3",
        "name": "iOS Version and Update Status",
        "when_to_ask": "For technical issues or before escalating",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(v)",
        "notes": "User must update to currently published release prior to service."
      },
      {
        "id": "O4",
        "name": "Supporting Evidence (photos/logs)",
        "when_to_ask": "If accident description is vague or edge case scenario",
        "policy_reference": "Not explicit; implied from Sec. 3.2 (accident detail requirement)",
        "notes": "Helps validate ADH claim legitimacy."
      },
      {
        "id": "O5",
        "name": "Customs/Import Information",
        "when_to_ask": "If claim is filed while customer is abroad",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 6.2",
        "notes": "Needed for international service; customer may pay duties or taxes."
      }
    ],
    "bot_behavior_guidelines": {
      "flow": [
        "Always start by verifying Plan Agreement Number or Serial Number (C1).",
        "Ask user to describe the issue (C2).",
        "Follow up with diagnostic questions (C3).",
        "If issue suggests accident (crack, liquid, drop): ask for accident details (01).",
        "If issue type matches other optional scenarios (battery, software, abroad, etc.): request corresponding optional info (O2–O5)."
      ],
      "tone": "Supportive, concise, guiding user step-by-step.",
      "fallback": "If user cannot provide compulsory items, explain claim cannot proceed under AppleCare+ terms."
    }
  }
}

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for contacting AppleCare. How may I help you today?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for contacting AppleCare. How may I help you today?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Hello! I need to file a claim for my iPhone. Had some trouble after a professional put on a screen protector. The adhesive seeped out and now the screen acts funny.
messages.raw_text: Hello! I need to file a claim for my iPhone. Had some trouble after a professional put on a screen protector. The adhesive seeped out and now the screen acts funny.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: I can assist you with that. To start, could you please provide your Plan Agreement Number or the serial number of your iPhone?
messages.raw_text: I can assist you with that. To start, could you please provide your Plan Agreement Number or the serial number of your iPhone?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Sure, my plan agreement number is APLCARE-5837291046, and the serial number of my iPhone is F3L9K5T7Q2W8.
messages.raw_text: Sure, my plan agreement number is APLCARE-5837291046, and the serial number of my iPhone is F3L9K5T7Q2W8.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for providing the details. Can you please describe the specific issue or symptoms you're experiencing with your iPhone?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for providing the details. Can you please describe the specific issue or symptoms you're experiencing with your iPhone?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Well, after they fixed the screen protector, the screen keeps acting up—sometimes it's unresponsive, and it's kinda weird. No drops or anything, just all this adhesive spill. It's pretty frustrating since I don’t usually have problems with my tech.
messages.raw_text: Well, after they fixed the screen protector, the screen keeps acting up—sometimes it's unresponsive, and it's kinda weird. No drops or anything, just all this adhesive spill. It's pretty frustrating since I don’t usually have problems with my tech.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for describing the issue. Is this problem related to hardware failure or screen damage caused by handling, or do you suspect it might be something else? Also, please tell me your iPhone model and iOS version.
messages.raw_text: Thank you for describing the issue. Is this problem related to hardware failure or screen damage caused by handling, or do you suspect it might be something else? Also, please tell me your iPhone model and iOS version.

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: It's definitely hardware, I think. The screen's acting strange, but I didn't drop it or get it wet. I have an iPhone 14 Pro Max, and I believe it's still running the latest iOS, but I’m not sure of the version exact. The whole situation just happened after some adhesive spill and reinstallation.
messages.raw_text: It's definitely hardware, I think. The screen's acting strange, but I didn't drop it or get it wet. I have an iPhone 14 Pro Max, and I believe it's still running the latest iOS, but I’m not sure of the version exact. The whole situation just happened after some adhesive spill and reinstallation.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for the information. Have you already tried updating your iOS to the latest release, or performing any other troubleshooting steps?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for the information. Have you already tried updating your iOS to the latest release, or performing any other troubleshooting steps?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: No, I haven't tried updating or anything. I'm not really into fiddling with settings or updates. I just want this fixed without messing around with tech stuff. This has been a bit of a hassle, you know?
messages.raw_text: No, I haven't tried updating or anything. I'm not really into fiddling with settings or updates. I just want this fixed without messing around with tech stuff. This has been a bit of a hassle, you know?

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Understood. To proceed, I will now ask if you have any supporting evidence, such as photos or logs, that could help validate your claim. Would you like to provide any?
messages.raw_text: Understood. To proceed, I will now ask if you have any supporting evidence, such as photos or logs, that could help validate your claim. Would you like to provide any?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Yeah, I took some pictures of the screen and the adhesive mess right after it happened. Not sure if they'll help, but I can send them over if needed.
messages.raw_text: Yeah, I took some pictures of the screen and the adhesive mess right after it happened. Not sure if they'll help, but I can send them over if needed.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: That would be very helpful. Please hold on while I record that you have supporting photos. Once I have all your information, I will process your claim. Is there anything else you would like to add or ask before we proceed?
messages.raw_text: That would be very helpful. Please hold on while I record that you have supporting photos. Once I have all your information, I will process your claim. Is there anything else you would like to add or ask before we proceed?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: No, I think that's all. Just want to get this sorted out. Thanks for helping me with this.
messages.raw_text: No, I think that's all. Just want to get this sorted out. Thanks for helping me with this.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: You're welcome. Your claim will be processed now. You will receive a notification as soon as it's done. If you need any further assistance, please let me know. Have a great day!
messages.raw_text: You're welcome. Your claim will be processed now. You will receive a notification as soon as it's done. If you need any further assistance, please let me know. Have a great day!

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Thanks, I appreciate it. Hope this gets resolved quickly. Have a good day!
messages.raw_text: Thanks, I appreciate it. Hope this gets resolved quickly. Have a good day!

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you, and have a great day as well! If you need further assistance in the future, don't hesitate to contact us.
messages.raw_text: Thank you, and have a great day as well! If you need further assistance in the future, don't hesitate to contact us. <END>
